headline: What Are the Biggest Mistakes to Avoid With Siding Replacement?
articleBody:
Replacing your siding is a major investment, and the last thing you want is to end up with problems down the road. Fortunately, avoiding the most common pitfalls is easy when you know what to look out for.

One of the biggest mistakes is failing to get the right permits. In communities across San Mateo County, replacing your siding almost always requires a building permit. It might seem like a hassle, but it's a huge protection for you. It ensures the work is done to code, and it prevents major issues when you try to sell your home later. An unpermitted siding job can be a deal-killer for buyers.

Another frequent issue is ignoring what's underneath. Simply covering up rotted sheathing or framing with beautiful new siding is a recipe for disaster. That hidden moisture and decay will continue to get worse, compromising the structure of your home and ruining your investment. A good contractor will always inspect and repair the substrate before installing anything new.

Finally, don't hire someone without the right license and insurance. If something goes wrong—from property damage to a worker getting injured—you could be on the hook. Working with a licensed, insured, and experienced professional is the single best way to protect yourself and ensure a high-quality result. At City Dweller, LLC, we manage the entire process correctly from start to finish. Call us at 650-532-5858 to learn more.
